items.title: Convert a flat project to src layout
items.scenario: Your repo has `ragapp/` next to `pyproject.toml`. Tests sometimes pass on your laptop but fail in CI.
items.explanation: Move `ragapp/` into `src/ragapp/`. Add `[tool.setuptools.packages.find] where = ['src']` to `pyproject.toml`. Run `pip install -e .`. Now `import ragapp` only works against the installed package — the same in CI and locally.

items.title: Catch a missing `__init__.py` early
items.scenario: You forgot to add `__init__.py` to a new sub-package. In a flat layout, it kind of worked thanks to namespace-package magic. Tests still passed.
items.explanation: With src/, the wheel built from `pip install -e .` actually exercises packaging; missing init files or unreachable modules show up immediately as `ImportError`, not as quiet runtime surprises later.

items.title: Run scripts that should not see internals
items.scenario: You have `scripts/build_index.py` that builds a search index. You don't want it to accidentally `from ragapp._internal import _private_helper`.
items.explanation: With src/, `scripts/` isn't on the import path; it can only `from ragapp import ...` via the installed package. Internals stay out of reach unless deliberately exported.

items.title: CI catches a missing dependency
items.scenario: Your code imports `requests` but `requests` is only installed because some other dev tool happens to have it as a transitive dep.
items.explanation: When CI does `pip install -e .` from a clean env, dependency gaps surface immediately — because only your declared deps are installed, and only those let `import ragapp` succeed. A flat layout would have hidden this until production.

items.title: Sub-package layout
items.explanation: Inside `src/ragapp/`, create directories `retrieval/`, `embeddings/`, `cli/`, each with their own `__init__.py`. The `src/` prefix is invisible at import time: callers write `from ragapp.retrieval import Retriever`.
